summary:
The patient has a 4 cm × 5 cm squamous cell carcinoma in the neck without evident primary site other than lymphadenopathy; based on current information, this likely stages as T2N1-2 due to its size and potential node involvement, though further details are needed for an accurate N category assignment. 
``` Summary: This patient has a 4 cm × 5 cm squamous cell carcinoma in the neck with no apparent primary site outside the lymph nodes; thus, he is most likely staged at least T2 according to his tumor size, although more detailed evaluation would refine whether the nodal status fits better into categories like N1 or potentially upstaging to N2 depending on specific findings such as multiple affected nodes. ```
